steps.title: Use the Kleenex comparison
steps.content: The text says Kleenex could become generic, as cellophane was. That comparison implies cellophane used to identify a particular company's product but later became a general term that other companies could use.

steps.title: Reject unsupported claims
steps.content:
• Choice A adds a legal debate over cellophane that the passage does not mention.
  • Choice C introduces recent popularity, which is not discussed.
  • Choice D conflicts with the comparison because becoming generic implies the term had trademark status before.
The answer is B.
